scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces generated text or document outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within specialized research tooling scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic generated text or document outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: Covers structured summary, priority ranking, and pending tasks; real-time monitoring and clinical diagnosis explicitly excluded. Timezone validation step added.

reliability.note: Timezone validation step added as step 3. Error handling documented; missing-field prompts present; clinical disclaimer prominently placed.

performance_context.note: No references/ directory; all content in single SKILL.md; event priority table is inline.

agent_usability.note: Workflow clear with timezone validation step. Priority levels well-defined; stress-case rules and response template present.

human_usability.note: Description is highly discoverable for clinical staff; forgiveness good — department filter is optional.

security.note: Clinical disclaimer present; HIPAA mentioned; no explicit PII detection or anonymization step before processing EHR data.

maintainability.note: Clean structure; event priority thresholds are inline — adjusting them requires editing SKILL.md.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision good; escape hatches present; composability moderate — JSON output option documented. Timezone validation improves reliability.

key_strengths: Timezone validation step now added as step 3 — shift times without timezone offset trigger an explicit UTC assumption warning
Clinical disclaimer is prominently placed at the top of SKILL.md, ensuring it appears in every output
Three-tier priority system (High/Medium/Low) with specific event type examples is well-calibrated for clinical handover
Explicit scope boundary prevents misuse for real-time monitoring or clinical diagnosis

recommendations.title: No PII/PHI detection or HIPAA warning before processing EHR data
recommendations.problem: The skill processes EHR records containing patient PII (name, DOB, MRN) without emitting any HIPAA warning or anonymization prompt, creating a data protection risk. This gap was not addressed in the polish round.
recommendations.root_cause: SKILL.md mentions HIPAA in the clinical disclaimer but provides no detection or enforcement logic for PII in input records.
recommendations.fix: Add a Sensitive Data Check step before processing. If input records contain patient identifiers, emit a mandatory HIPAA compliance warning and recommend verifying data handling authorization before proceeding.

recommendations.title: Event priority thresholds are inline — no configuration path for department-specific tuning
recommendations.problem: The event priority classification thresholds are defined inline in SKILL.md. The Scope Boundaries section notes that thresholds should be adjusted for department-specific needs, but no mechanism for doing so is provided.
recommendations.root_cause: No configuration file or parameter for custom priority thresholds is documented.
recommendations.fix: Move event priority definitions to a references/priority_thresholds.md or config file and document how to customize them for specific departments (e.g., ICU vs. general ward).
